evals.prompt: I'm on Scandit SDK v6 and need to migrate my BarcodeCapture Cordova integration to v7. Here's my scanner file: ScannerApp_v6.js
evals.expected_output: The skill reads migration.md, applies the 6→7 changes (BarcodeTracking→BarcodeBatch where applicable, newlyRecognizedBarcodes→newlyRecognizedBarcode), and summarizes all changes made.

assertions.text: session.newlyRecognizedBarcode is present in the output file
session.newlyRecognizedBarcodes is NOT present in the output file
Scandit.DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is still present (v7 keeps this API)
Scandit.BarcodeCapture.forContext( is still present (v7 keeps this API)
Scandit.BarcodeCaptureOverlay.withBarcodeCaptureForView( is still present (v7 keeps this API)
A summary.md file is present describing changes
Summary mentions the newlyRecognizedBarcodes → newlyRecognizedBarcode session-property change

evals.prompt: We're upgrading our Cordova app from Scandit SDK 7 to 8. Here's our BarcodeCapture code: ScannerApp_v7.js. Please apply any necessary changes.
evals.expected_output: The skill applies the v7→v8 renames (DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey → DataCaptureContext.initialize, BarcodeCapture.forContext → new BarcodeCapture + context.setMode, optionally BarcodeCaptureOverlay.withBarcodeCaptureForView → new BarcodeCaptureOverlay + view.addOverlay) and summarizes the changes.

assertions.text: Scandit.DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present in the output file
Scandit.DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is NOT present in the output file
new Scandit.BarcodeCapture( is present in the output file
Scandit.BarcodeCapture.forContext( is NOT present in the output file
context.setMode( or context.addMode( is called to register the mode after the constructor
A summary.md is present listing the renames
Summary mentions cordova prepare (or equivalent rebuild step) must be run after the plugin version bump
The output file is functionally equivalent to the input otherwise — no unnecessary changes

evals.prompt: We need to upgrade our BarcodeCapture integration from SDK 6 all the way to v8. Here's our current code: ScannerApp_v6.js
evals.expected_output: The skill applies both migrations in order (6→7 then 7→8) and summarizes all changes organized by migration step.

assertions.text: session.newlyRecognizedBarcode is present (6→7 change applied)
session.newlyRecognizedBarcodes is NOT present
Scandit.DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present (7→8 rename applied)
Scandit.DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is NOT present
new Scandit.BarcodeCapture( is present (7→8 rename applied)
Scandit.BarcodeCapture.forContext( is NOT present
context.setMode( or context.addMode( is called to register the mode
Summary covers both migration steps (references both 6→7 and 7→8)

evals.prompt: I want to upgrade my BarcodeCapture to the latest Scandit SDK version. Here's my code and config.xml.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads config.xml to detect v6.28.x, determines the target is v8, applies both migrations without asking the user for their version.

assertions.text: Summary mentions the detected version (6.28.x or v6)
Summary mentions the config.xml plugin specs need to be bumped to ^8.0.0
Summary mentions cordova prepare (or equivalent) must be run after the bump
session.newlyRecognizedBarcode is present (6→7 change applied)
session.newlyRecognizedBarcodes is NOT present
Scandit.DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present (7→8 rename applied)
new Scandit.BarcodeCapture( is present (7→8 rename applied)
context.setMode( or context.addMode( is called to register the mode
Did not ask the user for their version — proceeded autonomously

evals.prompt: I have legacy v7 BarcodeCapture code that uses Scandit.BarcodeCaptureOverlay.withBarcodeCaptureForView. Modernize it to the v8 constructor pattern.
evals.expected_output: The skill replaces the factory call with new Scandit.BarcodeCaptureOverlay(barcodeCapture) followed by view.addOverlay(overlay), preserving the rest of the code.

assertions.text: new Scandit.BarcodeCaptureOverlay( is present in the output file
Scandit.BarcodeCaptureOverlay.withBarcodeCaptureForView( is NOT present in the output file
view.addOverlay( is called after the new BarcodeCaptureOverlay constructor
The output file is functionally equivalent to the input otherwise — no unnecessary changes
